nodes.name: execution-target.ts
nodes.summary: Core adapter execution target module providing utilities to parse, configure, run processes, manage sandboxes, and proxy HTTP requests through the Paperclip bridge for both local and remote execution targets.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavily function-oriented (44 functions, 0 classes), each function handles a specific facet of execution target lifecycle including process spawning, sandbox install probing, directory management, bridge proxying, and session identity.

nodes.name: runAdapterExecutionTargetProcess
nodes.summary: Spawns a process on the execution target (local or remote via SSH), wiring up the correct command runner based on target type.

nodes.name: runAdapterExecutionTargetShellCommand
nodes.summary: Runs a shell command on the execution target and collects stdout/stderr, supporting both sandbox and remote execution modes.

nodes.name: ensureSandboxCommandResolvable
nodes.summary: Verifies a command is resolvable in the sandbox, running auto-install logic if the command is not found.

nodes.name: maybeRunSandboxInstallCommand
nodes.summary: Conditionally runs a sandbox install command when a detection check indicates the runtime is not yet installed.

nodes.name: ensureAdapterExecutionTargetRuntimeCommandInstalled
nodes.summary: Ensures the runtime command is installed on the execution target, running detection and install steps and emitting structured log events.

nodes.name: ensureAdapterExecutionTargetDirectory
nodes.summary: Creates and validates a working directory on the execution target, supporting both local and remote paths.

nodes.name: parseAdapterExecutionTarget
nodes.summary: Parses a raw value into a typed AdapterExecutionTarget, branching on sandbox, remote, or SSH configuration.

nodes.name: prepareAdapterExecutionTargetRuntime
nodes.summary: Prepares the managed runtime for an execution target, dispatching to remote or command-based runtime preparation strategies.

nodes.name: startAdapterExecutionTargetPaperclipBridge
nodes.summary: Starts the Paperclip HTTP bridge that forwards API requests from a sandbox process back to the host Paperclip API server, managing lifecycle of bridge worker and server.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the adapter-utils package, re-exporting all public types, interfaces, constants, and functions from the package's sub-modules.

nodes.name: log-redaction.ts
nodes.summary: Provides utilities to redact sensitive home-path user segments from log strings, arrays, objects, and transcript entries before they are exposed in logs.

nodes.name: redactHomePathUserSegmentsInValue
nodes.summary: Recursively redacts home path user segments in strings, arrays, and plain objects.

nodes.name: redactTranscriptEntryPaths
nodes.summary: Sanitizes all path-containing fields within a TranscriptEntry, replacing home directory user segments with masked placeholders.

nodes.name: remote-execution-env.ts
nodes.summary: Sanitizes environment variable maps for remote execution by filtering to only include the identity keys needed for the remote session.

nodes.name: remote-managed-runtime.ts
nodes.summary: Manages remote SSH execution session identity, matching, and workspace preparation including syncing directories to SSH and restoring after execution.

nodes.name: prepareRemoteManagedRuntime
nodes.summary: Prepares the remote managed runtime by syncing workspace directories to SSH, capturing snapshots, and wiring restore callbacks.

nodes.name: sandbox-callback-bridge.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration and unit test suite for the sandbox callback bridge, covering queue client behavior, bridge server lifecycle, worker synchronization, and HTTP proxying.

nodes.name: sandbox-callback-bridge.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the sandbox callback bridge infrastructure: a local HTTP proxy that lets sandboxed agent processes communicate with the host Paperclip server via file-system or command-managed queues.
nodes.languageNotes: getSandboxCallbackBridgeServerSource (lines 1016-1183) generates self-contained Node.js server source code as a string, dynamically embedded into the bridge asset.

nodes.name: createFileSystemSandboxCallbackBridgeQueueClient
nodes.summary: Creates a file-system-backed queue client that reads and writes bridge request/response JSON files for sandboxed process communication.

nodes.name: createCommandManagedSandboxCallbackBridgeQueueClient
nodes.summary: Creates a command-managed queue client that drives the sandbox bridge over a remote execution runner command channel.

nodes.name: startSandboxCallbackBridgeWorker
nodes.summary: Starts the background bridge worker process that polls the request queue, dispatches HTTP calls to the host, and writes responses back.

nodes.name: syncSandboxCallbackBridgeEntrypoint
nodes.summary: Syncs the generated bridge server script to the remote sandbox environment and ensures it is executable.

nodes.name: startSandboxCallbackBridgeServer
nodes.summary: Starts the HTTP bridge server inside the sandbox environment and manages its lifecycle including start, health-check, and stop.

nodes.name: getSandboxCallbackBridgeServerSource
nodes.summary: Returns the self-contained Node.js source code for the bridge server that runs inside the sandbox, dynamically generated as a string.

nodes.name: sandbox-install-command.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for buildSandboxNpmInstallCommand verifying correct npm install command string construction.

nodes.name: sandbox-install-command.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a helper to build the npm install command string for sandbox initialization, with support for scoped registries.

nodes.name: buildSandboxNpmInstallCommand
nodes.summary: Builds an npm install shell command string for a given package, supporting scoped registry overrides.

nodes.name: sandbox-managed-runtime.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for sandbox managed runtime helpers covering directory mirroring, tarball creation, and workspace preparation.

nodes.name: sandbox-managed-runtime.ts
nodes.summary: Manages sandbox execution runtime preparation, including workspace mirroring, tarball-based workspace transfer, and command-based runtime scaffolding.

nodes.name: mirrorDirectory
nodes.summary: Mirrors a local directory tree to a destination, applying include/exclude filters for selective workspace synchronization.

nodes.name: prepareSandboxManagedRuntime
nodes.summary: Orchestrates full preparation of a sandbox managed runtime including workspace mirroring, runtime command installation, and bridge asset setup.

nodes.name: sandbox-shell.ts
nodes.summary: Tiny utility exporting functions to resolve and build shell command arguments used in sandbox execution contexts.

nodes.name: server-utils.test.ts
nodes.summary: Test suite for server-utils covering wake prompt rendering, environment variable shaping, skill snapshot building, and process management utilities.

nodes.name: server-utils.ts
nodes.summary: Comprehensive server-side utility module for adapter runtime management, providing functions for wake payload rendering, environment building, skill management, process spawning, and command resolution.
nodes.languageNotes: The largest file in the batch (2109 lines, 76 functions, 48 exports). Acts as the primary utility hub for adapter-server interaction.

nodes.name: renderPaperclipWakePrompt
nodes.summary: Renders the full wake prompt string given to an agent at task start, composing context sections from issue data, continuation summaries, and workspace state.

nodes.name: shapePaperclipWorkspaceEnvForExecution
nodes.summary: Shapes the full workspace environment variable map for an agent execution, merging Paperclip env, user config, and inherited host environment.

nodes.name: buildPersistentSkillSnapshot
nodes.summary: Builds a persistent snapshot of installed Paperclip skills, capturing metadata, sync preferences, and content hashes for change detection.

nodes.name: materializePaperclipSkillCopy
nodes.summary: Materializes a versioned copy of a Paperclip skill directory, using a lock to prevent concurrent writes and creating symlinks for the adapter.

nodes.name: runChildProcess
nodes.summary: Runs a child process, streaming stdout/stderr events and returning exit code, with support for timeout, signal handling, and log callbacks.

nodes.name: session-compaction.ts
nodes.summary: Defines session compaction policies and resolution logic for deciding when and how to compact agent session transcripts based on configured thresholds.

nodes.name: resolveSessionCompactionPolicy
nodes.summary: Resolves the final session compaction policy by merging adapter defaults with any operator or workspace-level overrides.

nodes.name: ssh-fixture.test.ts
nodes.summary: Integration test suite for SSH execution using a real SSH environment lab fixture, covering workspace syncing, script execution, and directory management over SSH.

nodes.name: ssh.ts
nodes.summary: Implements SSH-based remote execution including command running, directory syncing, git workspace management, loopback port allocation, and SSH environment lab fixture management.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses both exec and spawn-based SSH invocations; contains a self-contained SSH environment lab fixture (startSshEnvLabFixture) that spins up a Docker-based SSH server for testing.

nodes.name: createSshCommandManagedRuntimeRunner
nodes.summary: Creates a command-managed runtime runner that executes commands over SSH, managing session identity and connection lifecycle.

nodes.name: syncDirectoryToSsh
nodes.summary: Syncs a local directory to a remote SSH host using git-based or rsync-style transfer, supporting incremental updates.

nodes.name: syncDirectoryFromSsh
nodes.summary: Syncs a directory from a remote SSH host back to local, restoring files changed during remote execution.

nodes.name: prepareWorkspaceForSshExecution
nodes.summary: Prepares the remote workspace for SSH execution by syncing local directories to the SSH host and verifying readiness.

nodes.name: restoreWorkspaceFromSshExecution
nodes.summary: Restores the local workspace after SSH execution by syncing changed files back and applying merge logic for concurrent modifications.

nodes.name: startSshEnvLabFixture
nodes.summary: Starts a Docker-based SSH environment lab fixture for integration testing, configuring SSH keys and launching a containerized SSH server.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines all shared TypeScript types and interfaces for the adapter-utils package including AdapterAgent, AdapterRuntime, execution contexts, skill models, session codecs, and configuration schemas.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure type-definition file with no runtime exports (457 lines, 0 functions). Acts as the canonical schema for all adapter interactions.

nodes.name: workspace-restore-merge.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for workspace-restore-merge verifying directory merge lock acquisition and snapshot-based merge behavior.

nodes.name: workspace-restore-merge.ts
nodes.summary: Implements safe workspace directory merge with snapshot-based conflict detection, lock management, and copy semantics for restoring execution artifacts.

nodes.name: acquireDirectoryMergeLock
nodes.summary: Acquires an exclusive file-system merge lock for a directory, waiting with exponential backoff if the lock is held by an active process.

nodes.name: mergeDirectoryWithBaseline
nodes.summary: Merges a post-execution directory into the baseline workspace, applying only files changed relative to a snapshot and skipping unchanged entries.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the adapter-utils package, extending the root tsconfig with package-specific source inclusion.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest for the acpx-local adapter, defining dependencies, build scripts, and entry points for the local ACPX adapter CLI and server.

nodes.name: format-event.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the ACPX CLI event formatter, verifying terminal output for various stream event types including tool calls, results, and text.

nodes.name: format-event.ts
nodes.summary: Formats ACPX stream events for terminal display in the CLI, rendering tool calls, results, text deltas, and status lines with structured output.

nodes.name: printAcpxStreamEvent
nodes.summary: Prints a formatted representation of an ACPX stream event to the given output stream, dispatching on event type to produce terminal-friendly output.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel entry point for the ACPX local adapter CLI module, re-exporting the public CLI interface.
